name: K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); CNCF project; no licensing costs

auth.notes: kubeconfig with service account tokens or certificates

performance.notes: Kubernetes API server overhead similar to full K8s; lower memory footprint (512MB vs 2GB+ for full K8s)

agent_readiness.notes: CNCF lightweight Kubernetes distribution. Single binary, 512MB RAM, runs on ARM. Certified Kubernetes — all kubectl commands work. Bundles containerd, CoreDNS, Traefik ingress, local-path provisioner, Flannel networking. 10x simpler installation than full Kubernetes. Popular for edge computing, IoT, Raspberry Pi clusters, and homelab Kubernetes. Rancher/SUSE backed.

gotchas: SQLite backend by default — requires etcd or external DB for HA
Bundled components (Traefik, Flannel) differ from enterprise K8s stacks
Networking plugin lock-in (Flannel default; others possible but more work)
Some enterprise K8s features unavailable or simplified
best_when: Edge computing and IoT Kubernetes clusters (ARM, low memory)
Homelab and development Kubernetes without full cluster overhead
CI/CD test environments needing real Kubernetes
Single-node or small cluster Kubernetes for resource-constrained environments
avoid_when: Large enterprise clusters needing full EKS/GKE feature sets
High-availability requirements (need external DB or embedded etcd)
When managed K8s (EKS, GKE, AKS) simplicity preferred
summary: K3s is the CNCF-certified lightweight Kubernetes distribution from Rancher/SUSE. Single binary, 512MB RAM minimum, ARM support. All kubectl commands work. Bundles containerd, Traefik, CoreDNS, Flannel. 10x simpler to install than upstream Kubernetes. Best for edge computing, IoT, homelabs, and CI/CD test environments needing real Kubernetes without full cluster overhead.
